rawContent: Task: 846-260409-173335 | Outcome: completed | Observation: Task 846-260409-173335 completed all 7 pipeline stages with 0 retries each (241s duration, full mode), updating PR #843. The verify stage reported FAIL due to PagesPageConfig type constraint violations in .next/dev/types/validator.ts (three TS2344 errors), yet the pipeline proceeded through review-fix and ship without blocking and shipped the code. This run matches the persistent pattern seen across 9+ consecutive prior runs where verify FAIL status does not prevent shipment. What went well: all stages completed with 0 retries indicating stable stage execution. What went wrong: the verify-to-ship gate remains broken, allowing code with failing typecheck validation to be automatically shipped. | Pattern: This is the tenth consecutive run (832, 841, 260409-111824, 840, 842, 844, two 419-reruns, 847, and now 846) exhibiting the identical flaw: verify stage reports FAIL due to PagesPageConfig type constraint violations or test failures, yet the pipeline proceeds through review-fix and ship without blocking. Nine prior retrospectives across two days recommended adding a conditional gate in ship to check verify exit status, but the flaw persists unchanged. The autofix stage cannot resolve this class of structural type errors, and the pipeline lacks a mechanism to halt when verify reports FAIL. | Suggestion: Implement the verifyExitCode gate in the ship stage template that was recommended in nine prior retrospectives without implementation. Add a check that reads the verify stage exit status and halts with an error when verify reports FAIL, preventing automatic shipment of code that failed typecheck validation. This requires a configuration change to the ship stage template, not another retrospective observation. | Flaw: verify → ship gate — Verify stage FAIL status does not prevent pipeline progression to ship stage | Stages: taskify:completed, plan:completed, build:completed, verify:completed, review:completed, review-fix:completed, ship:completed
